failure_modes.text: Distributed systems underperform when local actors have no clear rules, signal flow, or feedback from which order can emerge.
failure_modes.source_quote: Order can emerge from local interactions rather than heavy central control.
failure_modes.mitigation: Define the minimal rules, signal flows, and feedback loops that local actors need before expecting useful emergent coordination.

failure_modes.text: Leaders can smother adaptive coordination by over-scripting every local move that should be solved in place.
failure_modes.source_quote: The model explains how coherent order can arise without top-down command of every step.
failure_modes.mitigation: Identify which decisions should be local and which need central coordination: over-scripting kills adaptation, but under-specifying kills coherence.

failure_modes.text: Self-organization becomes an excuse for weak ownership when teams remove structure instead of designing the right local constraints.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when self-organization is treated as permission for no design or no accountability.
failure_modes.mitigation: Pair self-organization with explicit ownership: when removing top-down structure, replace it with local accountability and visible decision rights, not a vacuum.

premortem_questions.text: Which local rules or incentives are producing the large-scale pattern we keep observing?
premortem_questions.source_quote: The outcome results from local interactions among parts of the system.

premortem_questions.text: What minimum structure must stay in place so distributed coordination does not collapse into noise or drift?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Danger when the focus on emergence is misapplied as making it up as you go along.

premortem_questions.text: Where would central control help less than better feedback among local actors?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Order arises through interaction of parts within the system.

heuristics.text: Design local rules, incentives, and feedback so the larger pattern can organize itself.
heuristics.source_quote: Order emerges from the interaction of simpler parts within a system.

heuristics.text: Do not centralize decisions that the local environment can solve faster with the right signals.
heuristics.source_quote: The whole comes into existence through the integration and synthesis of many components.

heuristics.text: Keep self-organization paired with explicit feedback and accountability instead of romanticizing spontaneity.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger when self-organization is treated as permission for no design or no accountability.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the chunks on distributed rules, local signals, and anti-romanticized decentralization.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader network-governance sprawl beyond the clearest distributed-coordination moves.
open_questions: The source supports broader organizational design, but the Wave 2 surface stays on local-rule and feedback quality.

allies: The raw source is strongest with emergence, feedback-loops, and systems-thinking.
antagonists: The strongest conflicts are with over-centralization and with structure-free improvisation.
structured_tensions: The main tension is between autonomy and the minimum design needed for coherent order.
